desc: Make connections between texts and personal experiences, other texts, and the world
Analyse and evaluate connections between texts, personal experiences, and broader contexts
Synthesise and critically evaluate connections between texts, personal experiences, and societal issues
Identify ways in which literacy skills are used in daily life and across subjects
Explain how literacy skills support learning and communication in various contexts
Analyse how literacy skills are applied and transferred across disciplines and contexts
Identify and describe Indigenous ways of knowing and communicating in texts
Explain how Indigenous perspectives and ways of knowing are reflected in texts
Critically analyse how Indigenous knowledge and perspectives are represented and honoured in texts
Listen actively and respond with relevant questions or comments
Listen to understand, and demonstrate understanding through questions and responses

Apply knowledge of roots, prefixes, suffixes, and word families to determine word meaning

Apply a range of grammar conventions to improve clarity and effectiveness in writing
Apply grammar conventions with sophistication to enhance writing style
Identify and use basic spelling conventions and strategies

Apply spelling strategies and edit writing for conventions

Evaluate the logic, sequence, and relationships between ideas in complex texts

Critically analyse vocabulary choices, figurative language, and author's style
Identify the author's point of view in simple texts

Identify information in texts and begin to question its accuracy

Analyse how social and cultural contexts influence texts and responses

Apply the writing process with increasing independence and sophistication

Generate, analyse, and refine ideas using sophisticated planning strategies
Revise writing to improve clarity and add details with support
